controls.title: Control Environment
controls.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values and exercises oversight responsibility. Management establishes structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in pursuit of organizational objectives.
evidence: Board or management committee charters and meeting minutes demonstrating oversight of internal controls
Documented organizational chart with clearly defined roles, responsibilities, and reporting lines
Code of conduct or ethics policy acknowledged by all personnel with records of annual attestation
Performance evaluation records showing accountability measures tied to control objectives

sub_controls.title: COSO Principle 1 — Demonstrates Commitment to Integrity and Ethical Values
COSO Principle 2 — Exercises Oversight Responsibility
COSO Principle 3 — Establishes Structure, Authority, and Responsibility
COSO Principle 4 — Demonstrates Commitment to Competence
COSO Principle 5 — Enforces Accountability

controls.title: Communication and Information
controls.description: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal controls. The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
evidence: Log management policy and procedures defining collection, retention, and review requirements
Evidence of centralized log aggregation covering critical systems with defined retention periods
Internal communication records such as policy distribution logs, security bulletins, or town hall documentation
Information quality assessments or data governance procedures demonstrating information reliability

sub_controls.title: COSO Principle 13 — Uses Relevant Information
COSO Principle 14 — Communicates Internally
COSO Principle 15 — Communicates Externally

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.description: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to those objectives. The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyzes risks as a basis for determining how those risks should be managed.
evidence: Formal risk assessment documentation including risk register with likelihood, impact, and treatment decisions
Vulnerability scanning reports from authenticated scans run on an at least quarterly basis
Threat modeling or risk analysis artifacts tied to critical systems or data flows
Evidence of periodic risk assessment reviews and updates when significant changes occur

sub_controls.title: COSO Principle 6 — Specifies Suitable Objectives
COSO Principle 7 — Identifies and Analyzes Risk
COSO Principle 8 — Assesses Fraud Risk
COSO Principle 9 — Identifies and Analyzes Significant Change

controls.title: Monitoring of Controls
controls.description: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning. The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action.
evidence: SIEM platform configuration showing active correlation rules, dashboards, and alert thresholds
Security monitoring runbooks or procedures defining alert triage and escalation workflows
Records of internal control reviews or self-assessments with documented findings and remediation
Deficiency tracking log showing identified control gaps, owners, and closure evidence

sub_controls.title: COSO Principle 16 — Conducts Ongoing and/or Separate Evaluations
COSO Principle 17 — Evaluates and Communicates Deficiencies

controls.title: Control Activities
controls.description: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels. The entity also selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
evidence: Access control policy and procedures including provisioning, review, and de-provisioning workflows
Configuration management baseline documentation and system configuration standards
Configuration drift detection reports or change validation records for critical systems
Sample of access reviews demonstrating periodic recertification of user entitlements

sub_controls.title: COSO Principle 10 — Selects and Develops Control Activities
COSO Principle 11 — Selects and Develops General Controls Over Technology
COSO Principle 12 — Deploys Control Activities Through Policies and Procedures

controls.title: Logical and Physical Access Controls
controls.description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives. Physical access to facilities and protected information assets is restricted to authorized personnel.
evidence: Identity and access management platform configuration showing role-based access enforcement and least privilege
MFA enrollment reports demonstrating coverage across privileged and remote access accounts
Encryption-at-rest configuration or attestation for databases, file stores, and backup media
Access provisioning and de-provisioning workflow records including timely offboarding evidence

sub_controls.title: Implements Logical Access Security Measures
Prior to Issuing System Credentials, Registration and Authorization Processes
Role-Based Access Controls
Restricts Physical Access
Logical Access Security Measures for Protection Against Threats
Security Measures Against Threats Outside System Boundaries
Transmission of Data Restricted to Authorized Individuals
Prevents or Detects Unauthorized or Malicious Software

controls.title: System Operations
controls.description: The entity detects and monitors for new vulnerabilities and responds to security threats and incidents in a timely manner to meet the entity's objectives. Operational procedures ensure the continued security and availability of systems and information.
evidence: Endpoint protection platform deployment reports showing coverage and active protection status across all endpoints
Network monitoring alerts and incident tickets demonstrating active threat detection and response
Centralized log management system configuration showing collection scope, retention, and integrity controls
Incident response records showing detection-to-containment timelines and post-incident reviews

sub_controls.title: Uses Detection and Monitoring Procedures
Monitors System Components for Anomalies
Evaluates Security Events
Responds to Security Incidents
Identifies, Develops, and Implements Remediation Activities

controls.title: Change Management
controls.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its change management objectives. Security testing is integrated into the software development and change management lifecycle.
evidence: Change management policy and procedures including approval workflows, testing requirements, and rollback plans
Change request records with evidence of technical review, security approval, and post-implementation validation
Configuration management database or asset inventory showing baseline configurations and change history
Code repository records or pipeline artifacts showing static analysis scans integrated into the deployment process

sub_controls.title: Authorizes, Designs, Develops or Acquires, Configures, Documents, Tests, Approves, and Implements Changes to Infrastructure, Data, Software, and Procedures

controls.title: Risk Mitigation
controls.description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions and the use of vendors and business partners. Contingency and continuity plans are designed to mitigate the impact of significant threats.
evidence: Vendor management policy and third-party risk assessment records for critical service providers
Vendor contracts containing security requirements, SLAs, and audit rights provisions
Backup configuration documentation and successful backup completion logs covering critical systems
Business continuity or disaster recovery plan with evidence of periodic testing and tabletop exercises

sub_controls.title: Identifies, Selects, and Develops Risk Mitigation Activities
Assesses and Manages Risks Associated With Vendors and Business Partners
